chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P. No. S-397 of 2025 Date Orders with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objection flag ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 28-11-2025 Mr. Imtiaz Ali Mugheri, Advocate a/w petitioner. Mr. Munawar Ali Abbasi, Assistant A.G a/w SIP Ali Dost on behalf of SSP, Larkana and SIP Muhammad Haroon of PS Badeh. ************** Learned A.A.G files comments on behalf of the official respondents, which are taken on record. Mr. Abdul Qadir Gopang, learned Advocate, files power on behalf of the private respondents along with a statement supported by certain documents; copies thereof have been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioner. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that although the petitioner has been acquitted by the official respondents in FIR No.22/2024, registered under Sections 302, 34 PPC at Police Station Badeh, the official respondents are still harassing him at the behest of the private respondents. Conversely, learned counsel for the private respondents submits that civil and criminal litigations regarding agricultural land are pending between the parties. He submits that a complaint under the Illegal Dispossession Act has been filed by the petitioner’s side, whereas the private respondents have instituted a Suit for Specific Performance of Contract. He further submits that the private respondents have not harassed the petitioner nor do they intend to do so in future. Learned A.A.G submits that the official respondents are duty-bound to act strictly in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioner as and when required.

He further submits that the private respondents have not harassed the petitioner nor do they intend to do so in future. Learned A.A.G submits that the official respondents are duty-bound to act strictly in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioner as and when required. After going through the statements filed by the parties, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he would be satisfied if the petition is disposed of with directions ensuring that no harassment is caused to the petitioner. In view of the above, the petition is disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner. The private respondents are also directed not to act in any manner contrary to law, as affirmed in their statement. It is, however, clarified that the proceedings pertaining to the agricultural land pending before the competent forum shall be decided strictly on their own merits, without being influenced by any observation made in this order. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the concerned official respondents and learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, for compliance. Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
